key_insight: In PDE-constrained inverse problems, the best solver depends mainly on how the unknown is represented: grid-based fields tend to favor adjoint optimization, while neural representations align naturally with PINNs.
engineering_relevance: For computational mechanics and scientific ML pipelines, this paper gives a fair baseline for choosing between adjoints and PINNs in parameter estimation, closure modeling, and constitutive inference.
method: Common formulation with matched domains, PDEs, observations, regularization, optimizer, and precision
Benchmark comparison on Burgers, Darcy permeability, Allen--Cahn reaction, and Navier--Stokes viscosity inversion
PINN-warm-started adjoint strategy combines fast reconstruction with adjoint-level accuracy
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates both methods under identical problem setups across four inverse-problem benchmarks and reports method performance and cost tradeoffs.
watch_item: Results are sensitive to the chosen parameterization and implementation details, so fairness requires strict matching of formulations and optimization settings.
paper_title: Adjoint Method versus Physics-Informed Neural Networks in PDE-Constrained Inverse Problems
